allResults.question:
You plan to implement an incremental embedding maintenance method using change data capture (CDC) on dbs.Articles.

You need to ensure that only the columns required to generate the embeddings are captured. The solution must support querying net changes.

How should you complete the Transact-SQL script?
allResults.userAnswer: Captured columns: N'ArticleId, Title, Body' | supports_net_changes: 1 | First step (enable CDC): EXEC sys.sp_cdc_enable_db;
allResults.correctAnswer: Captured columns: N'ArticleId, Title, Body' | supports_net_changes: 1 | First step (enable CDC): EXEC sys.sp_cdc_enable_db;
allResults.topic: 3.1 Design and Implement Models and Embeddings
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: Capture ArticleId, Title, Body (columns needed for embedding generation). supports_net_changes = 1 to enable net change queries. Enable CDC at database level first.

allResults.question:
You have a SQL Server 2025 instance with no outbound connectivity. SalesDB supports a RAG pattern.

You need to generate embeddings inside SQL Server and ensure only AIApplicationUser can run embedding generation.

Which TWO actions should you perform? (Select 2)
allResults.topic: 3.1 Design and Implement Models and Embeddings
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: With no outbound connectivity, use ONNX runtime with local paths (D). Grant EXECUTE on the model project to restrict access to AIApplicationUser (C).

allResults.question:
You plan to change the embedding model from text-embedding-ada-002 to text-embedding-3-small. Existing rows already contain embeddings.

You need to implement the model change. Applications must be able to use VECTOR_SEARCH without runtime errors.

What should you do first?
allResults.topic: 3.1 Design and Implement Models and Embeddings
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: When switching embedding models, you must regenerate existing embeddings first so all vectors are in the same space, preventing runtime errors.

allResults.question:
Embeddings are generated by text-embedding-ada-002. The AI architect recommends switching to text-embedding-3-small. The security team requires Entra managed identities only (no API keys).

Which TWO actions should you include? (Select 2)
allResults.topic: 3.1 Design and Implement Models and Embeddings
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: Change the endpoint (new model requires different deployment endpoint) and regenerate all embeddings (new model produces different vectors).

allResults.question:
The embedding pipeline runs once per night, but documentation is edited throughout the day.

You need to ensure that embeddings are updated every time the underlying content changes, without a nightly batch process.

What should you include in the solution?
allResults.topic: 3.1 Design and Implement Models and Embeddings
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: Table triggers fire immediately on data changes, allowing real-time embedding updates without a batch process.

allResults.question:
[Case Study - Fabrikam - Q8 of 9]

When users consume data through the RAG pattern on ProcedureDocuments, they experience data retrieval delays.

You need to improve data retrieval performance and reduce the number of tokens per retrieval.

What should you implement?
allResults.topic: 3.1 Design and Implement Models and Embeddings
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: Chunking breaks large documents into smaller pieces, reducing tokens per retrieval and improving RAG performance.

allResults.question:
You have an Azure AI Search service and an index named hotels that includes a vector field named DescriptionVector.

You need to implement a hybrid search query that uses DescriptionVector and includes captions.

How should you complete the REST request body?
allResults.userAnswer: queryType: semantic | semanticConfiguration: hotels | captions: extractive
allResults.correctAnswer: queryType: semantic | semanticConfiguration: hotels | captions: extractive
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: Semantic queryType enables semantic ranking. The semanticConfiguration references the index name ('hotels'). Captions of type 'extractive' provide extracted highlights.

allResults.question:
You need to create a stored procedure that performs hybrid search on dbo.Products.

Requirements:
- Use approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) to retrieve the top 20 candidate products.
- Re-rank only the candidates that also match a full-text query.
- Generate the query embedding.

Complete the stored procedure with appropriate functions.
allResults.userAnswer: Generate the query embedding: AI_GENERATE_EMBEDDINGS | ANN retrieval (top 20 candidates): VECTOR_SEARCH | Full-text re-ranking: CONTAINSTABLE
allResults.correctAnswer: Generate the query embedding: AI_GENERATE_EMBEDDINGS | ANN retrieval (top 20 candidates): VECTOR_SEARCH | Full-text re-ranking: CONTAINSTABLE
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: AI_GENERATE_EMBEDDINGS creates the query vector. VECTOR_SEARCH performs ANN retrieval. CONTAINSTABLE does full-text filtering for re-ranking.

allResults.question:
Users report slow semantic search during peak usage. VECTOR_DISTANCE('cosine', @query_vector, embedding) is used. CPU spikes during peak times.

You need to reduce latency and CPU utilization.

What should you do?
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: A vector index with VECTOR_SEARCH using ANN dramatically improves performance compared to brute-force VECTOR_DISTANCE scans.

allResults.question:
You add semantic ranking to a hybrid search query on the hotels index. Some queries return fewer results than expected, and captions and answers are missing.

You need to complete the hybrid search request to include more documents when ranking and always include captions and answers.
allResults.userAnswer: k (vector queries): 50 | queryType: semantic | captions: extractive | answers: extractive
allResults.correctAnswer: k (vector queries): 50 | queryType: semantic | captions: extractive | answers: extractive
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: k=50 retrieves more documents. queryType=semantic enables semantic ranking. extractive captions and answers always provide the desired output.

allResults.question:
Analysts report difficulty finding similar vehicle incidents based on details such as weather, traffic conditions, and location. You need to generate embeddings to resolve the issue.

Which column should you use?

Columns available: vehicleLocation, incidentDescription, incidentType, SeverityScore
allResults.topic: 3.1 Design and Implement Models and Embeddings
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: Embeddings represent semantic meaning of rich textual content. incidentDescription is the free-text narrative field that captures weather, traffic, and location context. vehicleLocation is too narrow (geospatial). incidentType is categorical. SeverityScore is numeric — none are suitable primary sources for semantic embeddings.

allResults.question:
You need to implement a hybrid search query on a stores table (with description and embedding columns) that:
- Uses full-text search on description for keyword matching
- Returns top 20 results with a weighted formula: 60% vector distance + 40% full-text rank

How should you configure the query components?
allResults.userAnswer: Semantic query operator/function: VECTOR_DISTANCE and order by distance ascending | Keyword retrieval operator/function: CONTAINSTABLE on description and return ranked matches | Final ranking expression: order by (distance*0.6) + ((1.0 - RANK/1000.0)*0.4)
allResults.correctAnswer: Semantic query operator/function: VECTOR_DISTANCE and order by distance ascending | Keyword retrieval operator/function: CONTAINSTABLE on description and return ranked matches | Final ranking expression: order by (distance*0.6) + ((1.0 - RANK/1000.0)*0.4)
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: VECTOR_DISTANCE returns exact distance (lower=more similar). CONTAINSTABLE returns RANK 0-1000 (higher=more relevant). The formula normalizes RANK to 0-1 and inverts it so both components are lower-is-better for consistent ascending ordering.

allResults.question:
You need to enable similarity search to retrieve the most relevant health summary reports from an Embeddings (vector(1536)) column. The solution must minimize latency.

What should you include?
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: CREATE VECTOR INDEX creates an ANN index specifically for vector similarity search, dramatically reducing latency compared to exact k-NN scans. Standard nonclustered and full-text indexes are not designed for vector similarity operations.

allResults.question:
You have NotesEmbeddings and DescriptionEmbeddings tables (both vector(1536)) generated with different chunk sizes. You need to perform approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) queries across both. The solution must minimize the impact of different chunk sizes.

What should you use?
allResults.userAnswer: Function: VECTOR_SEARCH | Distance metric: cosine distance
allResults.correctAnswer: Function: VECTOR_SEARCH | Distance metric: cosine distance
allResults.domainName: Implement AI Capabilities in Database Solutions
allResults.explanation: VECTOR_SEARCH performs ANN retrieval using a vector index. Cosine distance compares direction/angle rather than magnitude, making it less sensitive to chunk size differences. It's the standard metric for text embeddings per Microsoft and Azure OpenAI guidance.
